UID: 101915747 | Because of my love for the short story by Clark Ashton Smith and the setting of Morimens, I worked on an OC of a Vulthoom-inspired Awakener! ❤️ I call her **Persephone**. Thank you for taking the time to look! Here is my [Google Document](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1I2Wci8VzSFPUCw2jLKA5PQ7Cf5ZJGO5pxEgx41k7G64/edit?usp=sharing) with her profile and other details.

# Basis
## Real-World Mythos
Persephone is inspired by Vulthoom of the titular short story by Clark Ashton Smith.

Like Arachne (reminiscent of Atlach-Nacha, also created by Clark Ashton Smith), Persephone uses a Greek mythological name that reflects her Mythos counterpart. Vulthoom is an alien plant-like entity that has nurtured a city in the subterranean depths of Mars, likened to Satan; Persephone is the Greek goddess of spring and vegetation/nature, as well as the Queen of the Underworld.

In the short story, Vulthoom is an exile from another advanced cosmos of its kind that rose to power on Mars and founded the underground city of Ravormos. Its primary method of control and communication is through this hallucinogenic fragrance emitted by a heated fossilized blossom. Vulthoom and its underlings go through thousand-year cycles of sleep and activity with the use of rare gases (Bottles of Sleep), and are able to do so due to their increased lifespan – granted by Vulthoom via an elixir of longevity. Ultimately, the Earthmen of the story delay Vulthoom's conquest of Earth by smashing the Bottles of Sleep and allowing the narcotic gases to permeate the city of Ravormos.

content:
## Backstory
Persephone was an alien being from another galaxy, fleeing from Dissolution in her home world via ethership. For whatever reason, her ship’s flight path and/or landing were flawed, and she crashed into Kadath: barrelling through the crust deep underground. When she woke, she found herself in an expansive subterranean hollow (the remnants of a long-dried riverbed) populated by a primitive species of giants, the Aihai — who now worshipped her as a savior/deity, in part due to her meteoric arrival and advanced technology. Persephone taught these peoples the basics of alchemy and shared her technological expertise, and over time, the flourishing subterranean civilization of Ravormos established itself — a city that predated even Lemuria.

While there were marked differences in biology between Persephone and her industrious, giant citizenry, she worked tirelessly to improve their living conditions and general quality of life. These works included various elixirs of longevity, incense, energy sources, artificial sunlight, and vast gardens. Some of the more striking differences between herself and the Aihai, however, were their respective lifespans and circadian rhythms; while the giants lived for ~100 years or so and had daily work-sleep cycles of 24 hours, Persephone’s kind seemed to live for million(s) of years and cycled through thousand-year periods of rest and activity. As such, she had dedicated followers caring for her during her long years of sleep — some were granted similarly long lives via elixir, some slept and woke with her by the assistance of rare narcotic gases kept in the well-guarded Bottles of Sleep. By uncorking just one amphora, those in the same room would fall into repose for just as long as Persephone.

For millenia, Ravormos prospered — until Dissolution finally took hold there, too. Its closed, cavernous structure and isolation meant that the plague rapidly decimated the kingdom. As this sickness ravaged her civilization, Persephone dedicated her waking hours to working on a cure for her subjects. Time passed; hope dwindled. Eventually, Persephone herself began to erode in both body and mind. After the agonizing realization that she was unable to do anything more, she granted her people one last gift: the perfume of a fossilized blossom from her home world. With the last of her strength, she recovered the final specimen from the wreckage of her ethership, and by heating the antholite, she induced Edenlike dreams and euphoria in the whole of Ravormos. As a native entity that shared the same origin, however, this fragrance had no effect on Persephone personally; she had to endure the suffering of Dissolution, and still had tasks to accomplish regardless. She made the journey through the empty streets to the now-solitary Bottles of Sleep — and smashed all three at once. Lulled into eternal slumber with the most beautiful of dreams, stillness now reigned over Ravormos. In futility, Persephone spent her final days still attempting to work out a cure for those that may yet come after her, before finally, cruelly, succumbing to Dissolution herself.

The ruins of her underground kingdom, and her Silver Core, would remain silent and undisturbed until the Keeper unearthed them.

## Characterization
For Persephone’s personality, I feel like she initially seems aloof, but is actually deeply caring and dedicated. Her face just doesn’t reflect her emotions well; perhaps due to her constitution as an inhuman alien. She can be described as a genius (and perhaps a mildly eccentric one). Persephone is entirely absorbed in alchemy and the recovery of rare materials: fossils, unique flora and fauna, and mythical minerals such as adamantine. She values technological progress and is an enthusiastic teacher when it comes to her favored subjects. Her periods of wakefulness seem more akin to mania, but she sleeps like the dead during times of rest — nothing will disturb or wake her until she naturally rouses. As an Awakener, these cycles are not nearly as long: a week or so, rather than a thousand years.

Persephone is exceptionally empathetic towards hybrids and not-quite-human populaces, which stems from her love for Aihais. For whatever reason, she does not seem fond of her own species; she was an exile from her kind, even before she fled their universe to escape Dissolution.

# (Sample) Profile
## Skills
**Bottles of Sleep**        Exalt
Consume all stacks of 🌫️Phantasmagoria and inflict (ATK×200%) 🫧Poison on all enemies. Choose “Repose” or “Tumult.”
⚠Repose: For each stack of 🌫️Phantasmagoria consumed, restore +3% of Max HP, stacking up to 10 stacks.
⚠Tumult: For each stack of 🌫️Phantasmagoria consumed, trigger +10% of 🫧Poison on all enemies, stacking up to 10 stacks.

1 **Strike**
Deal (ATK×20%) DMG, Persephone obtains 10 Aliemus.
⚠Quasar: Obtain 1 stack of 🌫️Phantasmagoria.

1 **Defense**
Gain (DEF×20%) Shield, Persephone obtains 10 Aliemus.
⚠Quasar: Obtain 1 stack of 🌫️Phantasmagoria.

2 **Ironic Inoculation**
Persephone gains 50 Aliemus.
⚠Rouse: Gain 1 stack of 🌫️Phantasmagoria at turn start. At the end of each turn, recover HP equal to 1% of all 🫧Poison damage dealt that turn (excludes trigger damage). ⚠Retain.

2 **Amber Philtre Euphoria**
Other Awakeners gain 10 Aliemus. Recover (CON×20%) HP and obtain (ATKx20%) Temporary 🗡STR.
⚠Quasar: Obtain 1 stack of 🌫️Phantasmagoria.

4 **Eau de Antholite**
Inflict (ATK×50%) stacks of 🫧Poison on all enemies, and trigger 50% of 🫧Poison on all enemies.
⚠Quasar: Obtain 1 stack of 🌫️Phantasmagoria. ⚠Retain. Prepare 1.

## Enlighten
**Subterranean Court**
Obtain 5 stacks of “Phantasmagoria” when the battle begins, and Poison inflicted by “Bottles of Sleep” additionally +20%.

**Equilibrium**
“Basic Strike” recovers HP equal to 10% of DMG dealt.
“Basic Defense” inflicts stacks of 🫧Poison on one random enemy equal to the amount of Shield gained.

**Ethership Arc**
“Quasar” triggers grant an additional stack of “Phantasmagoria.” The stacking limit for “Phantasmagoria” is increased to 15.

**Psyche Surge** 12
Level Cap Increase        → +10
⚡️ DMG Amplification    → +19.2
🔑 Keyflare Regen Level    → +14.4
Millenia of Silence
**Over-Exaltation**: “Bottles of Sleep” additionally enjoys a 2× STR bonus. Consuming “Phantasmagoria” simultaneously triggers the effects of “Repose” and “Tumult.”
Chimerical Transmutation
**Absolute Axiom**: Rouse “Ironic Inoculation” is enhanced: Triggers 100% of all Poison on enemies when played, and the “Phantasmagoria” cap increases to 20.

## Talents
**Alien Alchemy**: Talent Lv. 1/1
For every 1% DMG Amplification attribute Persephone has, the Base HP Recovery caused by “Repose,” “Ironic Inoculation,” and “Amber Philtre Euphoria” +0.5%.

## Archives
### Basic Info
**Name**:         Persephone
**Birthday**:         08-Nov
**Gender**:         Female
**Height**:         4'10”
**Weight**:         117lbs
**Gnostic Index**:     42
